title: festuca rubra subsp. eriantha
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. eriantha, also known as red fescue or glaucous fescue, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue-green foliage and reddish tint on the leaf sheaths and stem bases. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Festuca rubra subsp. eriantha have been reported to provide relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried plant material in tinctures or capsules, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and optimal usage.
